output:
Source used: [Tesla, Inc. 2025 Form 10-K, SEC](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1318605/000162828026003952/tsla-20251231.htm).

## 1. Business model and primary segments (with revenue mix if disclosed)

Tesla designs, develops, manufactures, sells and leases fully electric vehicles, energy generation and storage systems, and related AI/software, service, charging, insurance and financing offerings. It sells generally direct to customers. Source: Item 1, Business, p.2.

Reportable segments in FY2025:
- Automotive: $82.056bn revenue, 86.5% of total revenue; includes EV sales/leasing, regulatory credits, used vehicles, service, Supercharging, insurance, parts and merchandise. Source: Note 16, Segment Reporting, pp.92-93.
- Energy generation and storage: $12.771bn revenue, 13.5% of total revenue; includes design, manufacture, installation, sales/leasing of energy storage/generation products and related services/incentives. Source: Note 16, pp.92-93.

## 2. Geographic mix of revenue / customers (with percentages if disclosed)

FY2025 revenue by sales location:
- United States: $47.627bn, 50.2%.
- China: $20.962bn, 22.1%.
- Other international: $26.238bn, 27.7%.
- Total: $94.827bn.

Percentages calculated from disclosed revenue table. Source: Note 16, Geographic Areas, p.93.

## 3. Capital structure (key debt / equity instruments outstanding)

As of Dec. 31, 2025, Tesla had $16.513bn cash and cash equivalents and $27.546bn short-term investments. Source: Consolidated Balance Sheet, p.49.

Debt and finance leases: $1.640bn current and $6.736bn long-term carrying value; total debt unpaid principal was $8.177bn, with $6.429bn unused committed debt capacity. Source: Note 9, Debt, p.73.

Key debt instruments: $3.249bn automotive asset-backed notes, $4.288bn China Working Capital Facility, $397mn energy asset-backed notes, $240mn cash equity debt, and a $5.0bn senior unsecured RCF with no borrowings outstanding. Source: Note 9, pp.73-74.

Equity: 3.751bn common shares issued and outstanding at Dec. 31, 2025; preferred stock authorized but none issued; total stockholders’ equity $82.137bn. Source: Consolidated Balance Sheet, p.49.

## 4. Most recent year financial trajectory (revenue, EBITDA or operating income, net income — actual values)

FY2025 total revenue was $94.827bn, down from $97.690bn in FY2024. Source: Consolidated Statements of Operations, p.50.

FY2025 operating income was $4.355bn, down from $7.076bn in FY2024. Source: Consolidated Statements of Operations, p.50.

FY2025 net income was $3.855bn, down from $7.153bn in FY2024; net income attributable to common stockholders was $3.794bn. Source: Consolidated Statements of Operations, p.50.

## 5. Top three risk factors (one sentence each)

Product execution risk: Delays or cost overruns in developing, launching and ramping vehicles, autonomous products, services or features could harm Tesla’s brand, profitability and financial condition. Source: Item 1A, Risk Factors, p.12.

Demand and adoption risk: If EV demand, Tesla vehicle demand or adoption of autonomous driving/Robotaxi solutions develops more slowly than expected, results could be harmed. Source: Item 1A, pp.14-15.

Competition risk: Growing competition in EVs, self-driving technology, ride-hailing, software platforms and energy products could drive lower unit sales, price cuts, revenue shortfalls and market-share loss. Source: Item 1A, p.15.

## 6. Any disclosed ESG positioning or sustainability targets

Tesla positions its business around fully electric vehicles, clean energy adoption, energy storage/solar products, renewable-powered charging where possible, and workplace/human-capital initiatives. Source: Item 1, Business, pp.2, 5, 10-11.

Quantified ESG or sustainability targets: NOT DISCLOSED in the Form 10-K reviewed.

Source: [Equinix FY2025 Form 10-K / Annual Report](https://investor.equinix.com/sec-filings/all-sec-filings/content/0001101239-26-000075/EQIXAnnualRpt2025PRINT.pdf).

## 1. Business model and primary segments (with revenue mix if disclosed)

Equinix is a REIT and global digital infrastructure company operating carrier-neutral IBX data centers and xScale data centers, with one primary business line: design, build-out and operation of IBX data centers. It reports three geographic segments: Americas, EMEA and Asia-Pacific. (Item 1, pp. 5-7; Note 18, p. F-56)

FY2025 revenue mix: colocation $6.475B, interconnection $1.655B, managed infrastructure $466M, other recurring $143M, totaling recurring revenue of $8.739B, or 94.8% of total revenue; non-recurring revenue was $478M, or 5.2%. (Note 18, pp. F-56-F-57)

## 2. Geographic mix of revenue / customers (with percentages if disclosed)

FY2025 revenue by segment was Americas $4.111B, 44.6%; EMEA $3.130B, 34.0%; and Asia-Pacific $1.976B, 21.4%. (Note 18, p. F-56)

Country-level revenue: U.S. revenue was $3.6B, or 39.1% of total revenue; no other country exceeded 10% of total revenue. Customer geographic mix is NOT DISCLOSED; no single customer accounted for 10% or more of revenue or accounts receivable. (Note 18, p. F-57)

## 3. Capital structure (key debt / equity instruments outstanding)

Senior notes were $18.359B gross and $18.209B net, with $1.299B current and $16.910B long-term; notes span multiple USD, EUR, CHF, SGD, CAD and JPY series due 2026-2052 and are unsecured senior obligations. (Note 10, pp. F-37-F-40)

Other debt included $703M of mortgage and loans payable, including $673M of term loans; the 2022 credit facility included a $4.0B multicurrency revolver and a £500M term loan, with $31M letters of credit outstanding and about $4.0B available under the revolver. (Note 10, p. F-37)

Equity: 300M common shares authorized; 98.288M issued and 98.226M outstanding at Dec. 31, 2025; total stockholders’ equity was $14.153B. No preferred stock was outstanding. (Balance Sheet, p. F-3; Note 11, pp. F-40-F-41)

## 4. Most recent year financial trajectory (revenue, EBITDA or operating income, net income — actual values)

FY2025 / FY2024 / FY2023: revenue was $9.217B / $8.748B / $8.188B; operating income was $1.848B / $1.328B / $1.443B; consolidated net income was $1.348B / $814M / $969M. (Consolidated Statements of Operations, p. F-4)

## 5. Top three risk factors (one sentence each)

Power availability and cost risk: increased power procurement costs, prolonged outages, shortages or capacity constraints could harm operations and customer delivery. (Item 1A, Summary of Risk Factors, p. 3)

Infrastructure and cyber risk: physical infrastructure failures or cybersecurity breaches could disrupt services, reduce revenue and damage reputation. (Item 1A, Summary of Risk Factors, pp. 3-4)

Leverage and funding risk: substantial debt could constrain cash flow and flexibility, while inability to generate operating cash flow or obtain external financing could limit expansion. (Item 1A, Summary of Risk Factors, p. 4)

## 6. Any disclosed ESG positioning or sustainability targets

Equinix positions its ESG program as “Future First,” focused on sustainable digital infrastructure, customer sustainability reporting, resource efficiency and communities. (Sustainability Report Highlights, p. 6)

Disclosed targets include SBTi-verified goals to reach net-zero GHG emissions across the value chain by 2040, reduce absolute Scope 1 and 2 emissions 50% by 2030, reduce Scope 3 fuel- and energy-related emissions 50% by 2030, and achieve 100% clean and renewable energy coverage across retail IBX data centers by 2030; 2025 renewable energy coverage was 96%. (Sustainability Report Highlights, pp. 10-11)

Source filing: [Spotify Technology S.A. 2025 Form 20-F](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1639920/000162828026006874/ck0001639920-20251231.htm), fiscal year ended December 31, 2025.

## 1. Business model and primary segments (with revenue mix if disclosed)

Spotify is a global audio streaming platform with 751 million MAUs and 290 million Premium Subscribers across 184 countries and territories, offering music, podcasts, audiobooks, and selected video content. Its model combines paid subscriptions and advertising-supported usage. (Item 4.B, pp. 34-35; Item 5, p. 42)

Primary segments are Premium and Ad-Supported. In 2025, Premium generated €15.350 billion, or 89% of total revenue, primarily from subscription fees; Ad-Supported generated €1.836 billion, or 11%, primarily from display, audio, and video advertising. Total revenue was €17.186 billion. (Item 5, p. 47; Note 23, p. F-50)

## 2. Geographic mix of revenue / customers (with percentages if disclosed)

2025 revenue by country was United States €6.470 billion, Luxembourg €13 million, and Other countries €10.703 billion, out of total revenue of €17.186 billion. Implied mix: United States 37.6%, Luxembourg 0.1%, Other countries 62.3%; no country inside “Other countries” individually represented 10% or more of total revenue. (Note 23, p. F-50)

Customer/user geography was disclosed by MAUs: Europe 26%, North America 16%, Latin America 21%, and Rest of World 37% as of December 31, 2025. (Item 4.B, p. 34)

## 3. Capital structure (key debt / equity instruments outstanding)

Spotify’s ordinary shares, par value €0.000625, trade on the NYSE under `SPOT`; 205,832,527 ordinary shares were outstanding at December 31, 2025, with 209,485,215 issued and fully paid and 3,652,688 held in treasury. (Cover page; Note 16, pp. F-33 to F-34)

Debt consisted mainly of US$1.5 billion 0% Exchangeable Senior Notes due March 15, 2026, issued by Spotify USA Inc. and fully and unconditionally guaranteed by Spotify Technology S.A.; carrying/fair value at December 31, 2025 was €1.458 billion, classified as current. Lease liabilities totaled €498 million. (Notes 18 and 22, p. F-49; Statement of financial position, p. F-7; Note 10, p. F-29)

Spotify also had 309,932,980 beneficiary certificates outstanding, issued to founder-owned entities, with no economic rights but one vote each. (Item 7.A, pp. 78-79; Note 16, p. F-33)

## 4. Most recent year financial trajectory (revenue, EBITDA or operating income, net income — actual values)

For 2025, revenue was €17.186 billion, up 10% from €15.673 billion in 2024. Operating income was €2.198 billion, up from €1.365 billion in 2024. Net income attributable to owners of the parent was €2.212 billion, up from €1.138 billion in 2024. EBITDA was NOT DISCLOSED; operating income is disclosed. (Consolidated statement of operations, p. F-5; Item 5 revenue table, p. 47)

## 5. Top three risk factors (one sentence each)

Failure to attract, retain, and monetize users could reduce growth and revenue, especially because Ad-Supported users are a key source of Premium subscriber conversion. (Item 3.D, p. 5)

Competition from Apple, Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, ByteDance, and other media and advertising platforms could pressure user growth, engagement, advertising budgets, pricing, margins, and profitability. (Item 3.D, pp. 6-7)

Spotify depends on third-party content licenses, and adverse changes to licensing terms, royalty rates, renewals, or rights-holder access could materially harm results. (Item 3.D, pp. 12-13)

## 6. Any disclosed ESG positioning or sustainability targets

Spotify states that its environmental approach focuses on reducing GHG emissions and using its platform to support climate engagement among creators and listeners. It is currently evaluating its previously communicated goal of achieving net-zero emissions by 2030, including scope, timing, and methodology. No revised quantitative emissions target was disclosed in the Form 20-F. (Item 4.B, p. 40)
